comment: CSS server whose STORAGE BACKEND is the PGSL lattice (foundation-first Stage 5).
This IS the live interego-css config (it replaced the former file-backed
same import set — initialize-root + location/root (single root storage), allow-all
authorization (content privacy is Interego client-side JWE, NOT ACLs), websockets
notifications, memory resource-locker, suffix identifiers — with ONE change: the file
backend (css:config/storage/backend/file.json) is replaced by the PGSL accessor, which
re-platforms storage off the incoherent AzureFile SMB volume onto managed Postgres (the
documented unlock for CSS multi-replica; a coherent backend makes a distributed redis
locker viable, unlike the AzureFile file backend).
The Postgres connection string is read from PGSL_PG_CONNSTR by the factory, so no secret
lives in this file. NOTE: componentsjs-generator is BUILD-TIME ONLY — it must not be present
in the runtime node_modules (its newer @comunica/componentsjs collide with CSS's); the
Dockerfile prunes it (and tsx/typescript) from the runtime stage.

@graph.comment: Same DataAccessorBasedStore as the file backend, but with the PGSL accessor.
The PGSL-backed DataAccessor. connectionString omitted -> read from PGSL_PG_CONNSTR.
